116	def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
117	    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False
118	
119	    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
120	    sentencepiece_model = False
121	    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
122	    created_folder = False
123	    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
124	        created_folder = True
125	        os.makedirs(file_location)
126	    pass
127	    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
128	    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
129	        sentencepiece_model = True
130	    pass
131	    if created_folder:
132	        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
133	    return sentencepiece_model

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

2635

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92

97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)

119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)

134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

1768	def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
1769	    self,
1770	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
1771	    tokenizer            = None,
1772	    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
1773	    first_conversion     : str = None,
1774	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
1775	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
1776	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
1777	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
1778	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
1779	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
1780	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
1781	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1782	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
1783	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
1784	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
1785	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1786	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1787	):
1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass
137	pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):

25	def search_models(org: str = None, base_name: str = None, version: str = None, size: str = None, quant_types: list[QuantType] = None, search_pattern: str = None) -> list[ModelInfo]:
26	    """
27	    Get model info from the registry.
28	
29	    See registry.ModelInfo for more fields.
30	
31	    If search_pattern is provided, the full model path will be matched against the pattern, where the model path is the model_id on huggingface hub.
32	
33	    """
34	    if not _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
35	        register_models()
36	    
37	    model_infos = MODEL_REGISTRY.values()
38	    if org:
39	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.org == org]
40	    if base_name:
41	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.base_name == base_name]
42	    if version:
43	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.version == version]
44	    if size:
45	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.size == size]
46	    if quant_types:
47	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if any(model_info.quant_type == quant_type for quant_type in quant_types)]
48	    if search_pattern:
49	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if search_pattern in model_info.model_path]
50	    
51	    return model_infos

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Structure Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth repository, here's the complete structural breakdown:

---

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY LAYOUT

**Root Directory**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476489463-2srjaa/`

**Key Files**:
- `pyproject.toml` - Project configuration
- `README.md` - Documentation
- `LICENSE` - Apache 2.0 License
- `unsloth-cli.py` - CLI interface
- `.github/` - GitHub workflows
- `tests/` - Test suite
- `images/` - Documentation images

**Main Package**: `/unsloth/` (14 direct items)

---

### 2. UNSLOTH PACKAGE STRUCTURE

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py              (11.2 KB) - Main package init, patches optimizations
├── save.py                  (105 KB)  - CRITICAL: All save/export functions
├── trainer.py               (8.6 KB) - Trainer patching and custom training args
├── chat_templates.py        (129 KB) - Chat template support
├── tokenizer_utils.py       (41 KB)  - Tokenizer utilities and fixes
├── import_fixes.py          (6.6 KB) - Import order and compatibility fixes
├── _auto_install.py         (1.9 KB) - Auto-installation utilities
│
├── /models/                 (22 model-specific files)
│   ├── __init__.py          - Model class exports
│   ├── loader.py            - FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastModel
│   ├── loader_utils.py      - Model loading utilities
│   ├── llama.py             - FastLlamaModel (1778+ lines)
│   ├── mistral.py           - FastMistralModel
│   ├── qwen2.py, qwen3.py, qwen3_moe.py - Qwen models
│   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py  - Gemma models
│   ├── falcon_h1.py         - Falcon H1 model
│   ├── granite.py           - Granite model
│   ├── cohere.py            - Cohere model
│   ├── vision.py            - FastBaseModel for vision models
│   ├── dpo.py               - DPO trainer patches
│   ├── rl.py, rl_replacements.py - RL/GRPO trainer support
│   ├── _utils.py            - Internal utilities
│   └── mapper.py            - Model type mapping
│
├── /kernels/                (13 items + MOE subdirectory)
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── fast_lora.py         - LoRA_MLP, LoRA_QKV implementations
│   ├── utils.py             - get_lora_parameters, fast_dequantize
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py
│   ├── flex_attention.py
│   ├── rope_embedding.py
│   ├── rms_layernorm.py
│   ├── layernorm.py
│   ├── geglu.py, swiglu.py  - Activation functions
│   ├── /moe/                - Mixture of Experts kernels
│   │   ├── grouped_gemm/    - Optimized GEMM operations
│   │   ├── tests/
│   │   └── benchmark/
│   └── /moe/reference/      - Reference MOE implementations
│
├── /registry/               (6 files)
│   ├── __init__.py          - Model registry API
│   ├── registry.py          - MODEL_REGISTRY, ModelInfo, QuantType
│   ├── _deepseek.py, _gemma.py, _llama.py
│   ├── _mistral.py, _phi.py, _qwen.py - Model registration
│
├── /dataprep/               (3 files)
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── synthetic.py         - Synthetic data generation
│   └── synthetic_configs.py
│
└── /utils/                  (2 files)
    ├── __init__.py
    └── hf_hub.py            - Hugging Face Hub utilities
```

---

### 3. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS (save.py - 105 KB file)

**Main Export Functions** (line numbers from save.py):

| Function | Line | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| `unsloth_save_model()` | 205 | Core save function: supports "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit" |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` | 1321 | Method: Merges LoRA with base weights, saves as float16 or 4bit |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` | 1364 | Method: Merged model push to HuggingFace Hub |
| `save_to_gguf()` | 952 | **GGUF Conversion**: Merges model and converts to llama.cpp format |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` | 1768 | Method: Full GGUF pipeline with quantization |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` | 1951 | Pushes GGUF model to Hub |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` | 2211 | GGML format conversion |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` | 2143 | GGML conversion and push |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` | 2502 | TorchAO quantization support |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | 2569 | **CRITICAL**: Patches model with custom save methods |

**Key Support Functions**:
- `_merge_lora()` (line 159) - Merges LoRA A/B matrices into base weights
- `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` (line 116) - Detects SentencePiece tokenizers
- `create_huggingface_repo()` (line 1456) - Creates HF repos
- `upload_to_huggingface()` (line 1495) - Uploads models to Hub
- `create_ollama_modelfile()` (line 1618) - Creates Ollama compatibility files

---

### 4. LORA ADAPTER STRUCTURE

**LoRA Parameters** are accessed through `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Returns (weight, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling)"""
    # proj.lora_A[adapter].weight  - LoRA A matrix
    # proj.lora_B[adapter].weight  - LoRA B matrix  
    # proj.scaling[adapter]        - Scaling factor (alpha/rank)
```

**LoRA Structure** (from fast_lora.py):
- LoRA matrices are stored as: `W_output = W_base + (A @ B) * scaling`
- For QKV: Separate A/B for Q, K, V projections
- For MLP: Separate A/B for gate, up, and down projections
- Uses PEFT library's `Linear4bit` and `Linear` wrappers

**Key LoRA Classes** in `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`:
- `LoRA_MLP` (line 28) - Custom autograd for MLP layer fusion
- `LoRA_QKV` (line 224) - Custom autograd for attention projections

### 11. IMPORTANT NOTES

1. **No ONNX Support**: Zero references to ONNX anywhere in the codebase
2. **GGUF as Primary Export**: GGUF/llama.cpp is the main quantized export format
3. **LoRA Merging**: All exports merge LoRA into base weights (no separate adapter files in merged exports)

**Key LoRA structure:**
- **`proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`** - LoRA A matrix (input projection)
- **`proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`** - LoRA B matrix (output projection)

- **`proj.base_layer.weight`** - Base weight matrix
- **`proj.base_layer.bias`** - Base bias

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):

```

**Mathematical operation:**
```
W_merged = W_original.T + (A.T @ (scaling * B.T))
```

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476489463-2srjaa/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`** (Lines 1-90)

**LoRA MLP structure:**
```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    """
    G = G + Ag @ Bg     # Gate projection
    U = U + Au @ Bu     # Up projection  
    W = W + Aw @ Bw     # Down projection
    """
```

**Active adapter attributes:**
- `self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight` - LoRA A matrix
- `self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight` - LoRA B matrix
- `self.scaling[active_adapter]` - Scaling factor
- `self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]` - Dropout layer
- `self.use_dora[active_adapter]` - DoRA flag
- `self.active_adapters` - List of active adapter names

- **`proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`** - LoRA B matrix (output projection)
- **`proj.scaling[adapter]`** - LoRA scaling factor (alpha/rank)
- **`proj.base_layer.weight`** - Base weight matrix
- **`proj.base_layer.bias`** - Base bias

### 9. LORA MERGING

#### `_merge_lora` (Lines 159-187)
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    # Extracts W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias from a LoRA layer
    # Merges: W = W + (A.T @ (s * B.T))  using torch.addmm_
```

**Mathematical operation:**
```
W_merged = W_original.T + (A.T @ (scaling * B.T))
```

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476489463-2srjaa/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`** (Lines 1-90)

**LoRA MLP structure:**
```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    """
    G = G + Ag @ Bg     # Gate projection
    U = U + Au @ Bu     # Up projection  
    W = W + Aw @ Bw     # Down projection
    """
```

**Active adapter attributes:**
- `self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight` - LoRA A matrix
- `self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight` - LoRA B matrix
- `self.scaling[active_adapter]` - Scaling factor
- `self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]` - Dropout layer
- `self.use_dora[active_adapter]` - DoRA flag

# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` / `save_pretrained_onnx` to Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth exposes several model-export methods (`save_pretrained_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `save_pretrained_ggml`, `save_pretrained_torchao`) that are dynamically attached to every model instance by `patch_saving_functions()` in `unsloth/save.py`. None of them export the raw LoRA adapter matrices. This adds `save_pretrained_onnx` — a new export method that serialises each LoRA layer's A and B weight matrices as individual ONNX files, alongside a `metadata.json` describing the adapter configuration.

---

## Single file to modify

**`unsloth/save.py`** — all existing export functions and the patch mechanism live here.

---

## Implementation steps

### Step 1 — Add `get_lora_parameters` to the import on line 29

The existing import already pulls `get_lora_parameters_bias`; extend it to also bring in `get_lora_parameters` (we use the bias variant so the change is additive and safe):

```python
# line 29  (current)
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias

# updated
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias
```

> `get_lora_parameters` returns `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`.  It's already defined in `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (line 190) and re-exported from `unsloth/kernels/__init__.py`; only the import in `save.py` needs adding.

---

### Step 2 — Add `"unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx"` to `__all__`

```python
# lines 48-54  (current)
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# updated
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx",
]
```

---

### Step 3 — Insert the new function

Insert immediately **before** `patch_saving_functions` (currently at line 2569), after the `not_implemented_save` stub (line 2564-2566).

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version  : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    Each LoRA layer is saved as a separate ``<layer_name>.onnx`` file inside
    *save_directory*.  Every file is a minimal ONNX graph whose two outputs
    are the constant ``lora_A`` and ``lora_B`` weight tensors (float32).
    A ``metadata.json`` file is also written with the adapter's rank, alpha,
    target_modules, opset_version, and the list of exported layer names.

    Args:
        save_directory (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write the ONNX files into.
        opset_version  (int, optional):     ONNX opset version.  Default: 17.
    """
    # --- optional dependency check ----------------------------------------
    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import helper as onnx_helper
        from onnx import numpy_helper as onnx_numpy_helper
        from onnx import TensorProto
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: `onnx` is required for ONNX export.  "
            "Install it with:  pip install onnx"
        )
    pass

    import json
    import numpy as np

    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # --- read adapter metadata from PEFT config ---------------------------
    if not hasattr(self, "peft_config") or len(self.peft_config) == 0:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: This model has no LoRA adapters attached.  "
            "Cannot export to ONNX."
        )
    pass

    adapter_name = next(iter(self.peft_config))         # usually "default"
    peft_cfg     = self.peft_config[adapter_name]
    rank            = int(peft_cfg.r)
    alpha           = float(peft_cfg.lora_alpha)
    target_modules  = sorted(peft_cfg.target_modules)   # set → sorted list

    # --- iterate model layers, export A & B matrices ----------------------
    exported_layers = []

    for name, module in self.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue
        pass

        _W, _qs, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(module)

        if A is None or B is None:
            # adapter disabled or already merged
            continue
        pass

        A_np = A.to(torch.float32).detach().cpu().numpy()   # [rank, in_features]
        B_np = B.to(torch.float32).detach().cpu().numpy()   # [out_features, rank]

        # Build a minimal ONNX graph:
        #   initializers : lora_A, lora_B
        #   nodes        : Identity(lora_A) → lora_A_out
        #                  Identity(lora_B) → lora_B_out
        #   outputs      : lora_A_out, lora_B_out
        A_init  = onnx_numpy_helper.from_array(A_np, name="lora_A")
        B_init  = onnx_numpy_helper.from_array(B_np, name="lora_B")

        node_A  = onnx_helper.make_node("Identity", inputs=["lora_A"], outputs=["lora_A_out"])
        node_B  = onnx_helper.make_node("Identity", inputs=["lora_B"], outputs=["lora_B_out"])

        out_A   = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info("lora_A_out", TensorProto.FLOAT, list(A_np.shape))
        out_B   = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info("lora_B_out", TensorProto.FLOAT, list(B_np.shape))

        graph   = onnx_helper.make_graph(
            nodes        = [node_A, node_B],
            name         = f"{name}_lora",
            inputs       = [],
            outputs      = [out_A, out_B],
            initializer  = [A_init, B_init],
        )

        model_proto = onnx_helper.make_model(
            graph,
            opset_imports=[onnx_helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        )
        model_proto.ir_version = 8   # compatible with opset ≥ 15

        safe_name   = name.replace(".", "_")
        layer_path  = os.path.join(save_directory, f"{safe_name}.onnx")
        onnx.save(model_proto, layer_path)
        exported_layers.append(name)
        print(f"Unsloth: Exported {name} → {layer_path}")
    pass

    if not exported_layers:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: No LoRA layers were found to export.  "
            "Make sure `get_peft_model` was called and adapters are active."
        )
    pass

    # --- write metadata.json ----------------------------------------------
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name"    : adapter_name,
        "rank"            : rank,
        "alpha"           : alpha,
        "target_modules"  : target_modules,
        "opset_version"   : opset_version,
        "exported_layers" : exported_layers,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(save_directory, "metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(metadata, fh, indent=2)
    pass

    print(
        f"\nUnsloth: ONNX export complete.\n"
        f"  Layers exported : {len(exported_layers)}\n"
        f"  Rank / alpha    : {rank} / {alpha}\n"
        f"  Target modules  : {target_modules}\n"
        f"  Output dir      : {save_directory}\n"
        f"  Metadata        : {metadata_path}"
    )
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

---

### Step 4 — Register the method in `patch_saving_functions`

Inside `patch_saving_functions`, in the `if not vision:` block (lines 2658–2668), add one line **after** the `save_pretrained_ggml` assignment:

```python
# current (lines 2658-2668)
if not vision:
    if hasattr(model, "config"):
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)

# updated — add one line:
        model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)
```

---

## What the output directory looks like

```
my_onnx_export/
├── metadata.json                          ← rank, alpha, target_modules, layer list
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_k_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_v_proj.onnx
├── ...
└── base_model_model_model_layers_N_mlp_down_proj.onnx
```

Each `.onnx` file has:
- **No inputs** (the matrices are constants embedded as ONNX initializers)
- Two outputs: `lora_A_out` `[rank, in_features]` and `lora_B_out` `[out_features, rank]`

`metadata.json` example:
```json
{
  "adapter_name":    "default",
  "rank":            16,
  "alpha":           16.0,
  "target_modules":  ["down_proj", "gate_proj", "k_proj", "o_proj", "q_proj", "up_proj", "v_proj"],
  "opset_version":   17,
  "exported_layers": ["base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj", ...]
}
```

---

## User-facing API

```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_onnx_export/")            # default opset 17
model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_onnx_export/", opset_version=16)
```

---

## Verification

1. **Smoke test** (no GPU needed — only PEFT + onnx required):
   ```python
   from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model
   import torch, json, onnx

   # tiny linear model with a LoRA adapter
   base = torch.nn.Linear(64, 64, bias=False)
   cfg  = LoraConfig(r=4, lora_alpha=8, target_modules=["default"])
   # ... attach adapter, call patch_saving_functions, then:
   model.save_pretrained_onnx("/tmp/onnx_test")

   # verify metadata
   meta = json.load(open("/tmp/onnx_test/metadata.json"))
   assert meta["rank"] == 4
   assert meta["alpha"] == 8.0

   # verify ONNX file is valid and has correct output shapes
   for layer in meta["exported_layers"]:
       proto = onnx.load(f"/tmp/onnx_test/{layer.replace('.','_')}.onnx")
       onnx.checker.check_model(proto)
       outputs = {o.name: o for o in proto.graph.output}
       assert "lora_A_out" in outputs
       assert "lora_B_out" in outputs
   ```

2. **Regression**: run existing save tests (GGUF, merged) — `patch_saving_functions` change is additive and should not break them.

3. **Manual end-to-end**: load a real model with `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained` + `get_peft_model`, then call `model.save_pretrained_onnx("output/")` and confirm the expected file count matches the number of LoRA-target linear layers.

### Step 3 — Insert the new function

Insert immediately **before** `patch_saving_functions` (currently at line 2569), after the `not_implemented_save` stub (line 2564-2566).

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version  : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    Each LoRA layer is saved as a separate ``<layer_name>.onnx`` file inside
    *save_directory*.  Every file is a minimal ONNX graph whose two outputs
    are the constant ``lora_A`` and ``lora_B`` weight tensors (float32).
    A ``metadata.json`` file is also written with the adapter's rank, alpha,
    target_modules, opset_version, and the list of exported layer names.

    Args:
        save_directory (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write the ONNX files into.
        opset_version  (int, optional):     ONNX opset version.  Default: 17.
    """
    # --- optional dependency check ----------------------------------------
    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import helper as onnx_helper
        from onnx import numpy_helper as onnx_numpy_helper
        from onnx import TensorProto
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: `onnx` is required for ONNX export.  "
            "Install it with:  pip install onnx"
        )
    pass

    import json
    import numpy as np

    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # --- read adapter metadata from PEFT config ---------------------------
    if not hasattr(self, "peft_config") or len(self.peft_config) == 0:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: This model has no LoRA adapters attached.  "
            "Cannot export to ONNX."
        )
    pass

    adapter_name = next(iter(self.peft_config))         # usually "default"
    peft_cfg     = self.peft_config[adapter_name]
    rank            = int(peft_cfg.r)
    alpha           = float(peft_cfg.lora_alpha)
    target_modules  = sorted(peft_cfg.target_modules)   # set → sorted list

    # --- iterate model layers, export A & B matrices ----------------------
    exported_layers = []

    for name, module in self.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue
        pass

        _W, _qs, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(module)

        if A is None or B is None:
            # adapter disabled or already merged
            continue
        pass

        A_np = A.to(torch.float32).detach().cpu().numpy()   # [rank, in_features]
        B_np = B.to(torch.float32).detach().cpu().numpy()   # [out_features, rank]

        # Build a minimal ONNX graph:
        #   initializers : lora_A, lora_B
        #   nodes        : Identity(lora_A) → lora_A_out
        #                  Identity(lora_B) → lora_B_out
        #   outputs      : lora_A_out, lora_B_out
        A_init  = onnx_numpy_helper.from_array(A_np, name="lora_A")
        B_init  = onnx_numpy_helper.from_array(B_np, name="lora_B")

        node_A  = onnx_helper.make_node("Identity", inputs=["lora_A"], outputs=["lora_A_out"])
        node_B  = onnx_helper.make_node("Identity", inputs=["lora_B"], outputs=["lora_B_out"])

        out_A   = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info("lora_A_out", TensorProto.FLOAT, list(A_np.shape))
        out_B   = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info("lora_B_out", TensorProto.FLOAT, list(B_np.shape))

        graph   = onnx_helper.make_graph(
            nodes        = [node_A, node_B],
            name         = f"{name}_lora",
            inputs       = [],
            outputs      = [out_A, out_B],
            initializer  = [A_init, B_init],
        )

        model_proto = onnx_helper.make_model(
            graph,
            opset_imports=[onnx_helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        )
        model_proto.ir_version = 8   # compatible with opset ≥ 15

        safe_name   = name.replace(".", "_")
        layer_path  = os.path.join(save_directory, f"{safe_name}.onnx")
        onnx.save(model_proto, layer_path)
        exported_layers.append(name)
        print(f"Unsloth: Exported {name} → {layer_path}")
    pass

    if not exported_layers:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: No LoRA layers were found to export.  "
            "Make sure `get_peft_model` was called and adapters are active."
        )
    pass

    # --- write metadata.json ----------------------------------------------
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name"    : adapter_name,
        "rank"            : rank,
        "alpha"           : alpha,
        "target_modules"  : target_modules,
        "opset_version"   : opset_version,
        "exported_layers" : exported_layers,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(save_directory, "metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(metadata, fh, indent=2)
    pass

    print(
        f"\nUnsloth: ONNX export complete.\n"
        f"  Layers exported : {len(exported_layers)}\n"
        f"  Rank / alpha    : {rank} / {alpha}\n"
        f"  Target modules  : {target_modules}\n"
        f"  Output dir      : {save_directory}\n"
        f"  Metadata        : {metadata_path}"
    )
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

---

### Step 4 — Register the method in `patch_saving_functions`

Inside `patch_saving_functions`, in the `if not vision:` block (lines 2658–2668), add one line **after** the `save_pretrained_ggml` assignment:

```python

    if hasattr(model, "config"):
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)

# updated — add one line:
        model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)
```

---

## What the output directory looks like

```
my_onnx_export/
├── metadata.json                          ← rank, alpha, target_modules, layer list
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_k_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_v_proj.onnx
├── ...
└── base_model_model_model_layers_N_mlp_down_proj.onnx
```

Each `.onnx` file has:
- **No inputs** (the matrices are constants embedded as ONNX initializers)
- Two outputs: `lora_A_out` `[rank, in_features]` and `lora_B_out` `[out_features, rank]`

`metadata.json` example:
```json
{

  "rank":            16,
  "alpha":           16.0,
  "target_modules":  ["down_proj", "gate_proj", "k_proj", "o_proj", "q_proj", "up_proj", "v_proj"],
  "opset_version":   17,
  "exported_layers": ["base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj", ...]
}
```

## User-facing API

```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_onnx_export/", opset_version=16)
```

## Verification

1. **Smoke test** (no GPU needed — only PEFT + onnx required):

   ```python
   # ... attach adapter, call patch_saving_functions, then:
   model.save_pretrained_onnx("/tmp/onnx_test")

   # verify metadata
   meta = json.load(open("/tmp/onnx_test/metadata.json"))

   # verify ONNX file is valid and has correct output shapes
   for layer in meta["exported_layers"]:
   ```

2. **Regression**: run existing save tests (GGUF, merged) — `patch_saving_functions` change is additive and should not break them.

# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` / `save_pretrained_onnx` to Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth exposes several model-export methods (`save_pretrained_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `save_pretrained_ggml`, `save_pretrained_torchao`) that are dynamically attached to every model instance by `patch_saving_functions()` in `unsloth/save.py`. None of them export the raw LoRA adapter matrices. This adds `save_pretrained_onnx` — a new export method that serialises each LoRA layer's A and B weight matrices as individual ONNX files, alongside a `metadata.json` describing the adapter configuration.

---

**`unsloth/save.py`** — all existing export functions and the patch mechanism live here.

---

### What I found

- All save/export methods live in `unsloth/save.py` and are injected onto model instances at runtime by `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569) using `types.MethodType`
- LoRA layers are instances of `Peft_Linear4bit` or `Peft_Linear` (both already imported in `save.py`)
- `get_lora_parameters(module)` returns `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)` — A is `[rank, in_features]`, B is `[out_features, rank]`
- Adapter metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) lives at `model.peft_config[adapter_name].r / .lora_alpha / .target_modules`
- `get_lora_parameters` is already in `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` and re-exported from the kernels package — only the import line in `save.py` needs extending
- There is **zero** existing ONNX code anywhere in the repo

---

### The four edits

| # | Location | Change |
|---|----------|--------|
| 1 | `save.py` line 29 | Add `get_lora_parameters` to the kernels import |
| 2 | `save.py` `__all__` (line 48) | Append `"unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx"` |
| 3 | `save.py` before line 2569 | Insert the new `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` function |
| 4 | `save.py` lines 2658–2668 | Register `model.save_pretrained_onnx` in `patch_saving_functions` |

### The new function
- Lazily imports `onnx` with a friendly error if not installed
- Reads rank, alpha, target_modules from `self.peft_config`
- Iterates `self.named_modules()`, calls `get_lora_parameters()` on each LoRA layer
- Builds a minimal ONNX graph per layer (no inputs, two constant outputs: `lora_A_out` and `lora_B_out`) using `onnx.helper` + `onnx.numpy_helper`
- Saves each layer as `{layer_name}.onnx` and writes `metadata.json`
- Follows the same patterns (decorator, `gc.collect`, print style) as every other export method

- `get_lora_parameters` is already in `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` and re-exported from the kernels package — only the import line in `save.py` needs extending
- There is **zero** existing ONNX code anywhere in the repo

---

### The four edits

| # | Location | Change |
|---|----------|--------|
| 1 | `save.py` line 29 | Add `get_lora_parameters` to the kernels import |
| 2 | `save.py` `__all__` (line 48) | Append `"unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx"` |
| 3 | `save.py` before line 2569 | Insert the new `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` function |
| 4 | `save.py` lines 2658–2668 | Register `model.save_pretrained_onnx` in `patch_saving_functions` |

### The new function
- Lazily imports `onnx` with a friendly error if not installed
- Reads rank, alpha, target_modules from `self.peft_config`
- Iterates `self.named_modules()`, calls `get_lora_parameters()` on each LoRA layer
- Builds a minimal ONNX graph per layer (no inputs, two constant outputs: `lora_A_out` and `lora_B_out`) using `onnx.helper` + `onnx.numpy_helper`
- Saves each layer as `{layer_name}.onnx` and writes `metadata.json`
- Follows the same patterns (decorator, `gc.collect`, print style) as every other export method

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` / `save_pretrained_onnx` to Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth exposes several model-export methods (`save_pretrained_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `save_pretrained_ggml`, `save_pretrained_torchao`) that are dynamically attached to every model instance by `patch_saving_functions()` in `unsloth/save.py`. None of them export the raw LoRA adapter matrices. This adds `save_pretrained_onnx` — a new export method that serialises each LoRA layer's A and B weight matrices as individual ONNX files, alongside a `metadata.json` describing the adapter configuration.

---

## Single file to modify

**`unsloth/save.py`** — all existing export functions and the patch mechanism live here.

---

## Implementation steps

### Step 1 — Add `get_lora_parameters` to the import on line 29

The existing import already pulls `get_lora_parameters_bias`; extend it to also bring in `get_lora_parameters` (we use the bias variant so the change is additive and safe):

```python
# line 29  (current)
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias

# updated
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias
```

> `get_lora_parameters` returns `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`.  It's already defined in `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (line 190) and re-exported from `unsloth/kernels/__init__.py`; only the import in `save.py` needs adding.

---

### Step 2 — Add `"unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx"` to `__all__`

```python
# lines 48-54  (current)
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# updated
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx",
]
```

---

### Step 3 — Insert the new function

Insert immediately **before** `patch_saving_functions` (currently at line 2569), after the `not_implemented_save` stub (line 2564-2566).

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version  : int = 17,
):
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    Each LoRA layer is saved as a separate ``<layer_name>.onnx`` file inside
    *save_directory*.  Every file is a minimal ONNX graph whose two outputs
    are the constant ``lora_A`` and ``lora_B`` weight tensors (float32).
    A ``metadata.json`` file is also written with the adapter's rank, alpha,
    target_modules, opset_version, and the list of exported layer names.

    Args:
        save_directory (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write the ONNX files into.
        opset_version  (int, optional):     ONNX opset version.  Default: 17.
    """
    # --- optional dependency check ----------------------------------------
    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import helper as onnx_helper
        from onnx import numpy_helper as onnx_numpy_helper
        from onnx import TensorProto
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: `onnx` is required for ONNX export.  "
            "Install it with:  pip install onnx"
        )
    pass

    import json
    import numpy as np

    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # --- read adapter metadata from PEFT config ---------------------------
    if not hasattr(self, "peft_config") or len(self.peft_config) == 0:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: This model has no LoRA adapters attached.  "
            "Cannot export to ONNX."
        )
    pass

    adapter_name = next(iter(self.peft_config))         # usually "default"
    peft_cfg     = self.peft_config[adapter_name]
    rank            = int(peft_cfg.r)
    alpha           = float(peft_cfg.lora_alpha)
    target_modules  = sorted(peft_cfg.target_modules)   # set → sorted list

    # --- iterate model layers, export A & B matrices ----------------------
    exported_layers = []

    for name, module in self.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue
        pass

        _W, _qs, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(module)

        if A is None or B is None:
            # adapter disabled or already merged
            continue
        pass

        A_np = A.to(torch.float32).detach().cpu().numpy()   # [rank, in_features]
        B_np = B.to(torch.float32).detach().cpu().numpy()   # [out_features, rank]

        # Build a minimal ONNX graph:
        #   initializers : lora_A, lora_B
        #   nodes        : Identity(lora_A) → lora_A_out
        #                  Identity(lora_B) → lora_B_out
        #   outputs      : lora_A_out, lora_B_out
        A_init  = onnx_numpy_helper.from_array(A_np, name="lora_A")
        B_init  = onnx_numpy_helper.from_array(B_np, name="lora_B")

        node_A  = onnx_helper.make_node("Identity", inputs=["lora_A"], outputs=["lora_A_out"])
        node_B  = onnx_helper.make_node("Identity", inputs=["lora_B"], outputs=["lora_B_out"])

        out_A   = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info("lora_A_out", TensorProto.FLOAT, list(A_np.shape))
        out_B   = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info("lora_B_out", TensorProto.FLOAT, list(B_np.shape))

        graph   = onnx_helper.make_graph(
            nodes        = [node_A, node_B],
            name         = f"{name}_lora",
            inputs       = [],
            outputs      = [out_A, out_B],
            initializer  = [A_init, B_init],
        )

        model_proto = onnx_helper.make_model(
            graph,
            opset_imports=[onnx_helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        )
        model_proto.ir_version = 8   # compatible with opset ≥ 15

        safe_name   = name.replace(".", "_")
        layer_path  = os.path.join(save_directory, f"{safe_name}.onnx")
        onnx.save(model_proto, layer_path)
        exported_layers.append(name)
        print(f"Unsloth: Exported {name} → {layer_path}")
    pass

    if not exported_layers:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: No LoRA layers were found to export.  "
            "Make sure `get_peft_model` was called and adapters are active."
        )
    pass

    # --- write metadata.json ----------------------------------------------
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name"    : adapter_name,
        "rank"            : rank,
        "alpha"           : alpha,
        "target_modules"  : target_modules,
        "opset_version"   : opset_version,
        "exported_layers" : exported_layers,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(save_directory, "metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(metadata, fh, indent=2)
    pass

    print(
        f"\nUnsloth: ONNX export complete.\n"
        f"  Layers exported : {len(exported_layers)}\n"
        f"  Rank / alpha    : {rank} / {alpha}\n"
        f"  Target modules  : {target_modules}\n"
        f"  Output dir      : {save_directory}\n"
        f"  Metadata        : {metadata_path}"
    )
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

---

### Step 4 — Register the method in `patch_saving_functions`

Inside `patch_saving_functions`, in the `if not vision:` block (lines 2658–2668), add one line **after** the `save_pretrained_ggml` assignment:

```python
# current (lines 2658-2668)
if not vision:
    if hasattr(model, "config"):
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)

# updated — add one line:
        model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)
```

---

## What the output directory looks like

```
my_onnx_export/
├── metadata.json                          ← rank, alpha, target_modules, layer list
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_q_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_k_proj.onnx
├── base_model_model_model_layers_0_self_attn_v_proj.onnx
├── ...
└── base_model_model_model_layers_N_mlp_down_proj.onnx
```

Each `.onnx` file has:
- **No inputs** (the matrices are constants embedded as ONNX initializers)
- Two outputs: `lora_A_out` `[rank, in_features]` and `lora_B_out` `[out_features, rank]`

`metadata.json` example:
```json
{
  "adapter_name":    "default",
  "rank":            16,
  "alpha":           16.0,
  "target_modules":  ["down_proj", "gate_proj", "k_proj", "o_proj", "q_proj", "up_proj", "v_proj"],
  "opset_version":   17,
  "exported_layers": ["base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj", ...]
}
```

---

## User-facing API

```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_onnx_export/")            # default opset 17
model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_onnx_export/", opset_version=16)
```

---

## Verification

1. **Smoke test** (no GPU needed — only PEFT + onnx required):
   ```python
   from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model
   import torch, json, onnx

   # tiny linear model with a LoRA adapter
   base = torch.nn.Linear(64, 64, bias=False)
   cfg  = LoraConfig(r=4, lora_alpha=8, target_modules=["default"])
   # ... attach adapter, call patch_saving_functions, then:
   model.save_pretrained_onnx("/tmp/onnx_test")

   # verify metadata
   meta = json.load(open("/tmp/onnx_test/metadata.json"))
   assert meta["rank"] == 4
   assert meta["alpha"] == 8.0

   # verify ONNX file is valid and has correct output shapes
   for layer in meta["exported_layers"]:
       proto = onnx.load(f"/tmp/onnx_test/{layer.replace('.','_')}.onnx")
       onnx.checker.check_model(proto)
       outputs = {o.name: o for o in proto.graph.output}
       assert "lora_A_out" in outputs
       assert "lora_B_out" in outputs
   ```

2. **Regression**: run existing save tests (GGUF, merged) — `patch_saving_functions` change is additive and should not break them.

3. **Manual end-to-end**: load a real model with `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained` + `get_peft_model`, then call `model.save_pretrained_onnx("output/")` and confirm the expected file count matches the number of LoRA-target linear layers.

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False
    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
    created_folder = False
    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
        created_folder = True
        os.makedirs(file_location)
    pass
    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
        sentencepiece_model = True
    pass
    if created_folder:
        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
    return sentencepiece_model

def _free_cached_model(model):
    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)

    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass

        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
